Paragraph.Text: assets used principally in an active business carried on by the corporation or by an active business corporation that is related to the corporation;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: shares issued by or debt owing by other active business corporations that are related to the corporation; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: a combination of assets described in paragraphs (a) and (b).

Definition.Text: carrying value of the assets of a corporation at any time means the amount at which the assets of the corporation would be valued for the purpose of the corporation’s balance sheet as of that time if that balance sheet were prepared in accordance with generally accepted accounting principles used in Canada at that time, except that an asset of a corporation that is a share or debt issued by a related corporation is deemed to have a carrying value of nil.
common share means a share prescribed for the purpose of paragraph 110(1)(d).

MarginalNote.DefinedTermFr: arrangement admissible de mise en commun

Definition.Text: eligible pooling arrangement in respect of an individual means an agreement in writing made between the individual and another person or partnership (which other person or partnership is referred to in this definition and subsection (3) as the “investment manager”) where the agreement provides for

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the transfer of funds or other property by the individual to the investment manager for the purpose of making investments on behalf of the individual;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the purchase of eligible small business corporation shares with those funds, or the proceeds of a disposition of the other property, within 60 days after receipt of those funds or the other property by the investment manager; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the provision of a statement of account to the individual by the investment manager at the end of each month that ends after the transfer disclosing the details of the investment portfolio held by the investment manager on behalf of the individual at the end of that month and the details of the transactions made by the investment manager on behalf of the individual during the month.

MarginalNote.DefinedTermFr: société admissible exploitant une petite entreprise

Definition.Text: eligible small business corporation at any time means, subject to subsection (10), a corporation that, at that time, is a Canadian-controlled private corporation all or substantially all of the fair market value of the assets of which at that time is attributable to assets of the corporation that are

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: assets used principally in an active business carried on primarily in Canada by the corporation or by an eligible small business corporation that is related to the corporation;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: shares issued by or debt owing by other eligible small business corporations that are related to the corporation; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: a combination of assets described in paragraphs (a) and (b).

Definition.Text: eligible small business corporation share of an individual means a common share issued by a corporation to the individual if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: at the time the share was issued, the corporation was an eligible small business corporation; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: immediately before and after the share was issued, the total carrying value of the assets of the corporation and corporations related to it did not exceed $50,000,000.

Definition.Text: permitted deferral of an individual in respect of a qualifying disposition of the individual means the amount determined by the formula

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the lesser of the amount included in the description of H and the total of all amounts each of which is the qualifying cost to the individual of a replacement share in respect of the qualifying disposition;
is the qualifying portion of the individual’s proceeds of disposition from the qualifying disposition; and
is the qualifying portion of the individual’s capital gain from the qualifying disposition.

Definition.Text: qualifying cost to an individual of particular replacement shares of the individual in respect of a qualifying disposition of the individual that are shares of the capital stock of a particular eligible small business corporation means the lesser of

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the total of all amounts each of which is the cost to the individual of such a replacement share; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the amount by which $2,000,000 exceeds the total of all amounts each of which is the cost to the individual of a share that was a share of the capital stock of the particular eligible small business corporation or of a corporation related to it at the time the particular replacement shares were acquired and that was a replacement share of the individual in respect of another qualifying disposition.

Definition.Text: qualifying disposition of an individual (other than a trust) means, subject to subsection (9), a disposition of shares of the capital stock of a corporation where each such share disposed of was

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: an eligible small business corporation share of the individual;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: throughout the period during which the individual owned the share, a common share of an active business corporation; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: throughout the 185-day period that ended immediately before the disposition of the share, owned by the individual.

MarginalNote.DefinedTermEn: qualifying portion of a capital gain

MarginalNote.DefinedTermFr: partie admissible d’un gain en capital

Definition.Text: qualifying portion of a capital gain of an individual from a particular qualifying disposition of the individual means the amount determined by the formula

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the individual’s capital gain from the particular qualifying disposition, determined without reference to this section;
is the amount, if any, by which the total of

FormulaParagraph.Label: (a)
FormulaParagraph.Text: the total of all amounts each of which is the adjusted cost base to the individual of a share of a particular corporation that was the subject of the particular qualifying disposition (which adjusted cost base shall be determined immediately before the share was disposed of and without reference to this section), and

FormulaParagraph.Label: (b)
FormulaParagraph.Text: the total of all amounts each of which is the adjusted cost base to the individual of a share of the particular corporation or a corporation related to it at the time of the particular qualifying disposition that was the subject of another qualifying disposition (in respect of which a permitted deferral was deducted under this section by the individual) that occurred at or before the time of the particular qualifying disposition (which adjusted cost base shall be determined immediately before the share was disposed of and without reference to this section)

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the total of all amounts each of which is the adjusted cost base to the individual of a share of the particular corporation that was the subject of the particular qualifying disposition (which adjusted cost base shall be determined immediately before the share was disposed of and without reference to this section).

MarginalNote.DefinedTermEn: qualifying portion of the proceeds of disposition

MarginalNote.DefinedTermFr: partie admissible du produit de disposition

Definition.Text: qualifying portion of the proceeds of disposition of an individual from a qualifying disposition means the amount determined by the formula

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the individual’s proceeds of disposition from the qualifying disposition;
is the individual’s qualifying portion of the capital gain from the qualifying disposition; and
is the individual’s capital gain from the qualifying disposition, determined without reference to this section.

Definition.Text: replacement share of an individual in respect of a qualifying disposition of the individual in a taxation year means an eligible small business corporation share of the individual that is

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: acquired by the individual in the year or within 60 days after the end of the year, but not later than 120 days after the qualifying disposition occurred; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: designated by the individual in the individual’s return of income for the year to be a replacement share in respect of the qualifying disposition.

Subsection.Text: Where an individual has made a qualifying disposition in a taxation year,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the individual’s capital gain for the year from the qualifying disposition is deemed to be the amount by which the individual’s capital gain for the year from the qualifying disposition, determined without reference to this section, exceeds the individual’s permitted deferral in respect of the qualifying disposition;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in computing the adjusted cost base to the individual of a replacement share of the individual in respect of the qualifying disposition at any time after its acquisition, there shall be deducted the amount of the ACB reduction of the individual in respect of the replacement share; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: where the qualifying disposition was a disposition of a share that was a taxable Canadian property of the individual, the replacement share of the individual in respect of the qualifying disposition is deemed to be taxable Canadian property of the individual.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Special rule — re eligible pooling arrangements
Subsection.Text: Except for the purpose of the definition eligible pooling arrangement in subsection (1), any transaction entered into by an investment manager under an eligible pooling arrangement on behalf of an individual is deemed to be a transaction of the individual and not a transaction of the investment manager.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Special rule — re acquisitions on death
Subsection.Text: For the purpose of this section, a share of the capital stock of a corporation, acquired by an individual as a consequence of the death of a person who is the individual’s spouse, common-law partner or parent, is deemed to be a share that was acquired by the individual at the time it was acquired by that person and owned by the individual throughout the period that it was owned by that person, if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: where the person was the spouse or common-law partner of the individual, the share was an eligible small business share of the person and subsection 70(6) applied to the individual in respect of the share; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: where the person was the individual’s parent, the share was an eligible small business share of the parent and subsection 70(9.2) applied to the individual in respect of the share.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Special rule — re breakdown of relationships
Subsection.Text: For the purpose of this section, a share of the capital stock of a corporation, acquired by an individual from a person who was the individual’s former spouse or common-law partner as a consequence of the settlement of rights arising out of their marriage or common-law partnership, is deemed to be a share that was acquired by the individual at the time it was acquired by that person and owned by the individual throughout the period that it was owned by that person if the share was an eligible small business share of the person and subsection 73(1) applied to the individual in respect of the share.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Special rule — re eligible small business corporation share exchanges
Subsection.Text: For the purpose of this section, where an individual receives shares of the capital stock of a corporation that are eligible small business corporation shares of the individual (in this subsection referred to as the “new shares”) as the sole consideration for the disposition of shares issued by another corporation that were eligible small business corporation shares of the individual (in this subsection referred to as the “exchanged shares”), the new shares are deemed to have been owned by the individual throughout the period that the exchanged shares were owned by the individual if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: paragraph 85(1)(h) or subsection 85.1(3) or 87(4) applied to the individual in respect of the new shares; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the individual’s total proceeds of disposition of the exchanged shares was equal to the total of all amounts each of which was the individual’s adjusted cost base of an exchanged share immediately before the disposition.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Special rule — re active business corporation share exchanges
Subsection.Text: For the purpose of this section, where an individual receives common shares of the capital stock of a corporation (in this subsection referred to as the “new shares”) as the sole consideration for the disposition of common shares of another corporation (in this subsection referred to as the “exchanged shares”), the new shares are deemed to be eligible small business corporation shares of the individual and shares of the capital stock of an active business corporation that were owned by the individual throughout the period that the exchanged shares were owned by the individual, if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: paragraph 85(1)(h) or subsection 85.1(3) or 87(4) applied to the individual in respect of the new shares;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the total of the individual’s proceeds of disposition in respect of the disposition of the exchanged shares was equal to the total of the individual’s adjusted cost bases immediately before the disposition of such shares; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the disposition of the exchanged shares was a qualifying disposition of the individual.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Special rule — re carrying on an active business
Subsection.Text: For the purpose of the definitions in subsection (1), a property held at any particular time by a corporation that would, if this Act were read without reference to this subsection, be considered to carry on an active business at that time, is deemed to be used or held by the corporation in the course of carrying on that active business if the property (or other property for which the property is substituted property) was acquired by the corporation, at any time in the 36-month period ending at the particular time, because the corporation

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: issued a debt or a share of a class of its capital stock in order to acquire money for the purpose of acquiring property to be used in or held in the course of, or making expenditures for the purpose of, earning income from an active business carried on by it;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: disposed of property used or held by it in the course of carrying on an active business in order to acquire money for the purpose of acquiring property to be used in or held in the course of, or making expenditures for the purpose of, earning income from an active business carried on by it; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: accumulated income derived from an active business carried on by it in order to acquire property to be used in or held in the course of, or to make expenditures for the purpose of, earning income from an active business carried on by it.

Subsection.Text: A disposition of a common share of an active business corporation (in this subsection referred to as the “subject share”) by an individual that, but for this subsection, would be a qualifying disposition of the individual is deemed not to be a qualifying disposition of the individual unless the active business of the corporation referred to in paragraph (a) of the definition active business corporation in subsection (1) was carried on primarily in Canada

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: at all times in the period that began at the time the individual last acquired the subject share and ended at the time of disposition, if that period is less than 730 days; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in any other case, for at least 730 days in the period referred to in paragraph (a).

Subsection.Text: For the purpose of this section, an eligible small business corporation and an active business corporation at any time do not include a corporation that is, at that time,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a professional corporation;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a specified financial institution;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: a corporation the principal business of which is the leasing, rental, development or sale, or any combination of those activities, of real property owned by it; or

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: a corporation more than 50 per cent of the fair market value of the property of which (net of debts incurred to acquire the property) is attributable to real property.

Subsection.Text: In determining whether a share owned by an individual is an eligible small business corporation share of the individual, this Act shall be read without reference to section 48.1.
The permitted deferral of an individual in respect of a qualifying disposition of shares issued by a corporation (in this subsection referred to as “new shares”) is deemed to be nil where

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the new shares (or shares for which the new shares are substituted property) were issued to the individual or a person related to the individual as part of a series of transactions or events in which

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: shares of the capital stock of a corporation (in this subsection referred to as the “old shares”) were disposed of by the individual or a person related to the individual, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the paid-up capital of old shares or the adjusted cost base to the individual or to a person related to the individual of the old shares was reduced;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the new shares (or shares for which the new shares are substituted property) were issued by the corporation that issued the old shares or were issued by a corporation that, at or immediately after the time of issue of those shares, was a corporation that was not dealing at arm’s length with the corporation that issued the old shares; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: it is reasonable to conclude that one of the main reasons for the series of transactions or events or a transaction in the series was to permit the individual, persons related to the individual, or the individual and persons related to the individual to become eligible to deduct under subsection (2) permitted deferrals in respect of qualifying dispositions of new shares (or shares substituted for the new shares) the total of which would exceed the total that those persons would have been eligible to deduct under subsection (2) in respect of permitted deferrals in respect of qualifying dispositions of old shares.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) applies to dispositions that occur after February 27, 2000 except that, for dispositions that occurred after February 27, 2000 and before October 18, 2000,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the definition active business corporation in subsection 44.1(1) of the Act, as enacted by subsection (1), shall be read without reference to the words “subject to subsection (10)” and as if the reference to the words “carried on” in paragraph (a) of that definition were read as a reference to “carried on primarily in Canada”;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the definition eligible small business corporation in subsection 44.1(1) of the Act, as enacted by subsection (1), shall be read without reference to the words “subject to subsection (10)”;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the definition eligible small business corporation share in subsection 44.1(1) of the Act, as enacted by subsection (1), shall be read as follows:

Definition.Text: eligible small business corporation share of an individual means a common share issued by a corporation to the individual if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: at the time the share was issued, the corporation was an eligible small business corporation;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: immediately before the share was issued, the total carrying value of the assets of the corporation and corporations related to it did not exceed $2,500,000; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: immediately after the share was issued, the total carrying value of the assets of the corporation and corporations related to it did not exceed $10,000,000.

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: the definition qualifying cost in subsection 44.1(1) of the Act, as enacted by subsection (1), shall be read as if the reference to “$2,000,000” in paragraph (b) of that definition were read as a reference to “$500,000”;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: the definition qualifying disposition in subsection 44.1(1) of the Act, as enacted by subsection (1), shall be read without reference to the words “subject to subsection (9)”;

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: the definition qualifying portion of a capital gain in subsection 44.1(1) of the Act, as enacted by subsection (1), shall be read as if the reference to “$2,000,000” in paragraph (c) of the description of K in that definition were read as a reference to “$500,000”; and

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: section 44.1 of the Act, as enacted by subsection (1), shall be read without reference to subsections 44.1(9) and (10) of the Act, as enacted by subsection (1).

Subsection.Text: Subsection 45(1) of the Act is amended by striking out the word “and” at the end of paragraph (b), by adding the word “and” at the end of paragraph (c) and by adding the following after paragraph (c):

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: in applying this subsection in respect of a non-resident taxpayer, a reference to “gaining or producing income” shall be read as a reference to “gaining or producing income from a source in Canada”.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) applies after October 1, 1996.
The portion of subsection 46(1) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Where a taxpayer has disposed of a personal-use property (other than an excluded property disposed of in circumstances to which subsection 110.1(1), or the definition total charitable gifts, total cultural gifts or total ecological gifts in subsection 118.1(1), applies) of the taxpayer, for the purposes of this subdivision
The portion of subsection 46(2) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Where part only of property disposed of
Subsection.Text: Where a taxpayer has disposed of part of a personal-use property (other than a part of an excluded property disposed of in circumstances to which subsection 110.1(1), or the definition total charitable gifts, total cultural gifts or total ecological gifts in subsection 118.1(1), applies) owned by the taxpayer and has retained another part of the property, for the purposes of this subdivision
Section 46 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (4):

For the purpose of this section, excluded property of a taxpayer means property acquired by the taxpayer, or by a person with whom the taxpayer does not deal at arm’s length, in circumstances in which it is reasonable to conclude that the acquisition of the property relates to an arrangement, plan or scheme that is promoted by another person or partnership and under which it is reasonable to conclude that the property will be the subject of a gift to which subsection 110.1(1), or the definition total charitable gifts, total cultural gifts or total ecological gifts in subsection 118.1(1), applies.
Subsections (1) to (3) apply to property acquired after February 27, 2000.
Section 47 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (2):

For the purpose of subsection (1), a security (within the meaning assigned by subsection 7(7)) acquired by a taxpayer after February 27, 2000 is deemed not to be identical to any other security acquired by the taxpayer if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the security is acquired in circumstances to which any of subsections 7(1.1), (1.5) or (8) or 147(10.1) applies; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the security is a security to which subsection 7(1.31) applies.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) applies after 1999.
Subparagraph 48.1(1)(a)(ii) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: immediately after that time, ceases to be a small business corporation because a class of its or another corporation’s shares is listed on a prescribed stock exchange, and

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) applies to corporations that cease to be small business corporations after 1999.

Where a corporation ceases to be a Canadian-controlled private corporation in a taxation year solely because of the application of subsection 113(2) of this Act, an election under subsection 48.1(1) of the Act, as enacted by subsection (1), that is made by an individual in respect of the 1999 or 2000 taxation year is deemed to have been made on time if the election is made on or before the individual’s filing-due date for the taxation year in which this Act receives royal assent.
Paragraph 49(5)(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: for the purposes of subsections (2) to (4) and subparagraph (b)(iv) of the definition disposition in subsection 248(1), the original option and each extension or renewal of it is deemed to be the same option; and

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) applies to options granted after December 23, 1998.
Subsections 52(1) and (1.1) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Cost of certain property the value of which included in income

Subsection.Text: Where

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a taxpayer acquired property after 1971 (other than an annuity contract, a right as a beneficiary under a trust to enforce payment of an amount by the trust to the taxpayer, property acquired in circumstances to which subsection (2) or (3) applies or property acquired from a trust in satisfaction of all or part of the taxpayer’s capital interest in the trust), and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: an amount in respect of its value was

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: included, otherwise than under section 7, in computing

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: the taxpayer’s taxable income or taxable income earned in Canada, as the case may be, for a taxation year during which the taxpayer was non-resident, or

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: the taxpayer’s income for a taxation year throughout which the taxpayer was resident in Canada, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: for the purpose of computing the tax payable under Part XIII by the taxpayer, included in an amount that was paid or credited to the taxpayer,

ContinuedSectionSubsection.Text: for the purposes of this subdivision, the amount so included shall be added in computing the cost to the taxpayer of the property, except to the extent that the amount was otherwise added to the cost or included in computing the adjusted cost base to the taxpayer of the property.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 52(6) of the Act is repealed.

Subsection (1) applies after 1999 except that, in respect of property acquired before 2000 and disposed of before March 2000, paragraph 52(1)(a) of the Act, as enacted by that subsection, shall be read as follows:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a taxpayer acquired property after 1971 (other than an annuity contract or property acquired as described in subsection (2), (3) or (6)), and

Subsection.Text: Subsection (2) applies after 1999, but not to rights that were acquired before 2000 and disposed of before March 2000.
Clauses 53(1)(e)(i)(A) and (A.1) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: the fractions set out in subsection 14(5), paragraphs 38(a) to (a.2), subsection 41(1) and in the formula in paragraph 14(1)(b),

Clause.Label: (A.1)
Clause.Text: paragraph 18(1)(l.1),

Clause.Label: (A.2)
Clause.Text: the description of C in the formula in paragraph 14(1)(b), and

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 53(1)(j) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.MarginalNote: Share or fund unit taxed as stock option benefit
Paragraph.Label: (j)
Paragraph.Text: if the property is a security (within the meaning assigned by subsection 7(7)) and, in respect of its acquisition by the taxpayer, a benefit was deemed by section 7 to have been received in any taxation year that ends after 1971 and begins before that time by the taxpayer or by a person that did not deal at arm’s length with the taxpayer or, if the security was acquired after February 27, 2000, would have been so deemed if section 7 were read without reference to subsections 7(1.1) and (8), the amount of the benefit that was, or would have been, so deemed to have been received;

Subsection.Text: Subparagraph (ii) of the description of A in paragraph 53(1)(r) of the Act is amended by replacing the reference to the expression “4/3 of” with a reference to the word “twice”.

Paragraph 53(2)(a) of the Act is amended by striking out the word “and” at the end of subparagraph (iii), by adding the word “and” at the end of subparagraph (iv) and by adding the following after subparagraph (iv):

Subparagraph.Label: (v)
Subparagraph.Text: any amount required by paragraph 44.1(2)(b) to be deducted in computing the adjusted cost base to the taxpayer of the share;

Subsection.Text: Clauses 53(2)(c)(i)(A) and (A.1) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: the fractions set out in subsection 14(5), paragraph 38(b) and in the formula in paragraph 14(1)(b),

Clause.Label: (A.1)
Clause.Text: paragraph 18(1)(l.1),

Clause.Label: (A.2)
Clause.Text: the description of C in the formula in paragraph 14(1)(b),

Subsection.Text: Clause 53(2)(c)(ii)(B) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: the Canadian exploration and development expenses and foreign resource pool expenses, if any, incurred by the partnership in the fiscal period,

Subsection.Text: The portion of paragraph 53(2)(h) of the Act before subparagraph (i) is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (h)
Paragraph.Text: where the property is a capital interest of the taxpayer in a trust (other than an interest in a personal trust that has never been acquired for consideration or an interest of a taxpayer in a trust described in any of paragraphs (a) to (e.1) of the definition trust in subsection 108(1)),

Subsection.Text: Subclause 53(2)(h)(i.1)(B)(I) of the Act is amended by striking out the reference to the expression “1/3 of”.
The portion of paragraph 53(2)(i) of the Act before subparagraph (i) is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (i)
Paragraph.Text: where the property is a capital interest in a trust (other than a unit trust) not resident in Canada that was purchased after 1971 and before that time by the taxpayer from a non-resident person at a time (in this paragraph referred to as the “purchase time”) when the property was not taxable Canadian property and the fair market value of such of the trust property as was

Subsection.Text: The portion of paragraph 53(2)(i) of the Act after subparagraph (v) is replaced by the following:

ContinuedParagraph.Text: was not less than 50% of the fair market value of all the trust property, that proportion of the amount, if any, by which

Subparagraph.Label: (vi)
Subparagraph.Text: the fair market value at the purchase time of such of the trust properties as were properties described in any of subparagraphs (i) to (v)

Subparagraph.Label: (vii)
Subparagraph.Text: the total of the cost amounts to the trust at the purchase time of such of the trust properties as were properties described in any of subparagraphs (i) to (v),

ContinuedParagraph.Text: that the fair market value at the purchase time of the interest is of the fair market value at the purchase time of all capital interests in the trust;

Subsection.Text: The portion of paragraph 53(2)(j) of the Act before subparagraph (i) is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (j)
Paragraph.Text: where the property is a unit of a unit trust not resident in Canada that was purchased after 1971 and before that time by the taxpayer from a non-resident person at a time (in this paragraph referred to as the “purchase time”) when the property was not taxable Canadian property and the fair market value of such of the trust property as was

Subsection.Text: The portion of paragraph 53(2)(j) of the Act after subparagraph (v) is replaced by the following:

ContinuedParagraph.Text: was not less than 50% of the fair market value of all the trust property, that proportion of the amount, if any, by which

Subparagraph.Label: (vi)
Subparagraph.Text: the fair market value at the purchase time of such of the trust properties as were properties described in any of subparagraphs (i) to (v)

Subparagraph.Label: (vii)
Subparagraph.Text: the total of the cost amounts to the trust at the purchase time of such of the trust properties as were properties described in any of subparagraphs (i) to (v),

ContinuedParagraph.Text: that the fair market value at the purchase time of the unit is of the fair market value at the purchase time of all the issued units of the trust;

Subsection.Text: Subsection 53(3) of the Act is repealed.
The portion of subsection 53(4) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Recomputation of adjusted cost base on transfers and deemed dispositions
Subsection.Text: Where at any time in a taxation year a person or partnership (in this subsection referred to as the “vendor”) disposes of a specified property and the proceeds of disposition of the property are determined under paragraph 48.1(1)(c), section 70 or 73, subsection 85(1), paragraph 87(4)(a) or (c) or 88(1)(a), subsection 97(2) or 98(2), paragraph 98(3)(f) or (5)(f), subsection 104(4), paragraph 107(2)(a), (2.1)(a), (4)(d) or (5)(a), 107.4(3)(a) or 111(4)(e) or section 128.1,

Subsections (1) and (5) apply in respect of fiscal periods that end after February 27, 2000 and, for fiscal periods that ended after February 18, 1997 and before February 28, 2000, clause 53(1)(e)(i)(A) of the Act, as enacted by subsection (1), shall be read as follows:

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: the fractions set out in subsection 14(5), paragraphs 38(a) and (a.1) and subsection 41(1),

Subsection.Text: Subsection (2) applies after 1999.

Subsection (3) applies to taxation years that end after February 27, 2000 except that, in applying paragraph 53(1)(r) of the Act, as enacted by subsection (3), for those years in respect of a taxpayer’s interest in an entity, where a taxation year of the entity that includes February 28, 2000 or October 17, 2000, or began after February 28, 2000 and ended before October 17, 2000, ends in the taxpayer’s taxation year, the reference to the word “twice” in subparagraph (ii) of the description of A in that paragraph shall be read as a reference to the expression “the fraction that is the reciprocal of the fraction in paragraph 38(a), as enacted by subsection 22(1) of the Income Tax Amendments Act, 2000, that applies in respect of the entity for its taxation year, multiplied by”.
Subsection (4) applies to dispositions that occur after February 27, 2000.
Subsection (6) applies to taxation years that begin after 2000.
Subsection (7) applies to amounts that become payable after 1999.

Subsection (8) applies to taxation years that end after February 27, 2000 except that, in applying subclause 53(2)(h)(i.1)(B)(I) of the Act, as enacted by subsection (8), for those years in respect of a taxpayer’s interest in a trust, where a taxation year of the trust that includes February 28, 2000 or October 17, 2000, or began after February 28, 2000 and ended before October 17, 2000, ends in the taxpayer’s taxation year, the reference to the expression “that is equal to the” in that subclause shall be read as a reference to the expression “that is equal to the fraction obtained when 1 is subtracted from the reciprocal of the fraction in paragraph 38(a), as enacted by subsection 22(1) of the Income Tax Amendments Act, 2000, that applies to the trust for its taxation year, multiplied by”.
Subsections (9) to (12) apply for the purpose of computing the adjusted cost base of property after April 26, 1995.
Subsection (13) applies after October 1, 1996.
Subsection (14) applies to the 1998 and subsequent taxation years.
The definition disposition in section 54 of the Act is repealed.
Paragraph (c) of the definition principal residence in section 54 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: where the taxpayer is an individual other than a personal trust, unless the particular property was designated by the taxpayer in prescribed form and manner to be the taxpayer’s principal residence for the year and no other property has been designated for the purposes of this definition for the year

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: where the year is before 1982, by the taxpayer, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: where the year is after 1981,

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: by the taxpayer,

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: by a person who was throughout the year the taxpayer’s spouse or common-law partner (other than a spouse or common-law partner who was throughout the year living apart from, and was separated under a judicial separation or written separation agreement from, the taxpayer),

Clause.Label: (C)
Clause.Text: by a person who was the taxpayer’s child (other than a child who was at any time in the year a married person, a person who is in a common-law partnership or 18 years of age or older), or

Clause.Label: (D)
Clause.Text: where the taxpayer was not at any time in the year a married person, a person who is in a common-law partnership or 18 years of age or older, by a person who was the taxpayer’s

Subclause.Label: (I)
Subclause.Text: mother or father, or

Subclause.Label: (II)
Subclause.Text: brother or sister, where that brother or sister was not at any time in the year a married person, a person who is in a common-law partnership or 18 years of age or older,

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) applies to transactions and events that occur after December 23, 1998.

Subsection (2) applies to dispositions that occur after 1990 except that clauses (c)(ii)(B) to (D) of the definition principal residence in section 54 of the Act, as enacted by subsection (2), shall be read without reference to “or common-law partner” and “a person who is in a common-law partnership” in their application to dispositions made by a taxpayer that occur in a taxation year that is before 2001 and

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: before 1998; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: after 1997, unless a valid election is made by the taxpayer under section 144 of the Modernization of Benefits and Obligations Act, that that Act apply to the taxpayer in respect of one or more taxation years that include the year.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 55(1) of the Act is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:

Definition.Text: specified corporation in relation to a distribution means a distributing corporation

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: that is a public corporation or a specified wholly-owned corporation of a public corporation,

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: shares of the capital stock of which are exchanged for shares of the capital stock of another corporation (referred to in this definition and subsection (3.02) as an “acquiror”) in an exchange to which the definition permitted exchange in this subsection would apply if that definition were read without reference to paragraph (a) and subparagraph (b)(ii) of that definition,

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: that does not make a distribution, to a corporation that is not an acquiror, after 1998 and before the day that is three years after the day on which the shares of the capital stock of the distributing corporation are exchanged in a transaction described in paragraph (b), and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: no acquiror in relation to which makes a distribution after 1998 and before the day that is three years after the day on which the shares of the capital stock of the distributing corporation are exchanged in a transaction described in paragraph (b),

ContinuedDefinition.Text: and, for the purposes of paragraphs (c) and (d),

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: a corporation that is formed by an amalgamation of two or more other corporations is deemed to be the same corporation as, and a continuation of, each of the other corporations, and

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: where there has been a winding-up of a corporation to which subsection 88(1) applies, the parent is deemed to be the same corporation as, and a continuation of, the subsidiary;

MarginalNote.DefinedTermFr: filiale à cent pour cent déterminée

Definition.Text: specified wholly-owned corporation of a public corporation means a corporation all of the outstanding shares of the capital stock of which (other than directors’ qualifying shares and shares of a specified class) are held by

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the public corporation,

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a specified wholly-owned corporation of the public corporation, or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: any combination of corporations described in paragraph (a) or (b).

Subsection.Text: Section 55 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (3.01):

For the purposes of the definition distribution in subsection (1), where the transfer referred to in that definition is by a specified corporation to an acquiror described in the definition specified corporation in subsection (1), the references in the definition distribution to

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: “each type of property” shall be read as “property”; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: “property of that type” shall be read as “property”.

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 55(5)(b) of the Act is amended by striking out the word “and” at the end of subparagraph (ii) and by replacing subparagraph (iii) with the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: the total of all amounts each of which is an amount required to have been included under this subparagraph as it read in its application to a taxation year that ended before February 28, 2000,

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: the amount, if any, by which

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: 1/2 of the total of all amounts each of which is an amount required by paragraph 14(1)(b) to be included in computing the corporation’s income in respect of a business carried on by the corporation for a taxation year that is included in the period and that ended after February 27, 2000 and before October 18, 2000,

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: where the corporation has deducted an amount under subsection 20(4.2) in respect of a debt established by it to have become a bad debt in a taxation year that is included in the period and that ended after February 27, 2000 and before October 18, 2000, or has an allowable capital loss for such a year because of the application of subsection 20(4.3), the amount determined by the formula

FormulaDefinition.Text: is 1/2 of the value determined for A under subsection 20(4.2) in respect of the corporation for the last such taxation year that ended in the period, and
is 1/3 of the value determined for B under subsection 20(4.2) in respect of the corporation for the last such taxation year that ended in the period, and

Clause.Label: (C)
Clause.Text: in any other case, nil, and

Subparagraph.Label: (v)
Subparagraph.Text: the amount, if any, by which

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: the total of all amounts each of which is an amount required by paragraph 14(1)(b) to be included in computing the corporation’s income in respect of a business carried on by the corporation for a taxation year that is included in the period and that ends after October 17, 2000,

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: where the corporation has deducted an amount under subsection 20(4.2) in respect of a debt established by it to have become a bad debt in a taxation year that is included in the period and that ends after October 17, 2000, or has an allowable capital loss for such a year because of the application of subsection 20(4.3), the amount determined by the formula

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the value determined for A under subsection 20(4.2) in respect of the corporation for the last such taxation year that ended in the period, and
is 1/3 of the value determined for B under subsection 20(4.2) in respect of the corporation for the last such taxation year that ended in the period, and

Clause.Label: (C)
Clause.Text: in any other case, nil;

Subsection.Text: The portion of paragraph 55(5)(e) of the French version of the Act before subparagraph (i) is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: e)
Paragraph.Text: pour déterminer si des personnes sont liées entre elles, si une personne est un actionnaire déterminé d’une société et si le contrôle d’une société a été acquis par une personne ou un groupe de personnes, les règles suivantes s’appliquent :

Subsection.Text: Subparagraph 55(5)(e)(iv) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: this Act shall be read without reference to subsection 251(3) and paragraph 251(5)(b); and

Subsection.Text: Subsections (1) and (2) apply to transfers that occur after 1998.
Subsection (3) applies in respect of taxation years that end after February 27, 2000.

Subsections (4) and (5) apply to dividends that are received after November 1999, other than dividends received as part of a transaction or event, or a series of transactions or events, that was required before December 1, 1999 to be carried out pursuant to a written agreement made before that day.
The portion of paragraph 56(1)(n) of the Act after subparagraph (i) is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the taxpayer’s scholarship exemption for the year computed under subsection (3);

Subsection.Text: Section 56 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (2):

Subsection.MarginalNote: Exemption for scholarships, fellowships, bursaries and prizes
Subsection.Text: For the purpose of subparagraph (1)(n)(ii), a taxpayer’s scholarship exemption for a taxation year is the greatest of

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the lesser of

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the total of all amounts each of which is the amount included under subparagraph (1)(n)(i) in computing the taxpayer’s income for the year in respect of a scholarship, fellowship or bursary received in connection with the taxpayer’s enrolment in an educational program in respect of which an amount may be deducted under subsection 118.6(2) in computing the taxpayer’s tax payable under this Part for the year, and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the total of all amounts each of which is the lesser of

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the amount included under subparagraph (1)(n)(i) in computing the taxpayer’s income for the year in respect of a scholarship, fellowship, bursary or prize that is to be used by the taxpayer in the production of a literary, dramatic, musical or artistic work, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the total of all amounts each of which is an expense incurred by the taxpayer in the year for the purpose of fulfilling the conditions under which the amount described in subparagraph (i) was received, other than

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: personal or living expenses of the taxpayer (except expenses in respect of travel, meals and lodging incurred by the taxpayer in the course of fulfilling those conditions and while absent from the taxpayer’s usual place of residence for the period to which the scholarship, fellowship, bursary or prize, as the case may be, relates),

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: expenses for which the taxpayer is entitled to be reimbursed, and

Clause.Label: (C)
Clause.Text: expenses that are otherwise deductible in computing the taxpayer’s income.

Subsection.Text: Subsections (1) and (2) apply to the 2000 and subsequent taxation years.
Subsection 59(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:
Where a taxpayer has disposed of a foreign resource property, there shall be included in computing the taxpayer’s income for a taxation year the amount, if any, by which

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the portion of the taxpayer’s proceeds of disposition from the disposition of the property that becomes receivable in the year

ContinuedSectionSubsection.Text: exceeds

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the total of

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: all amounts each of which is an outlay or expense made or incurred by the taxpayer for the purpose of making the disposition that was not otherwise deductible for the purposes of this Part, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: where the property is a foreign resource property in respect of a country, the amount designated under this subparagraph in prescribed form filed with the taxpayer’s return of income for the year in respect of the disposition.

Subsection.Text: Where a taxpayer is a member of a partnership in a fiscal period of the partnership, the taxpayer’s share of the amount that would be included under subsection (1) in respect of a disposition of a foreign resource property in computing the partnership’s income for a taxation year if the partnership were a person, the fiscal period were a taxation year, subsection (1) were read without reference to subparagraph (1)(b)(ii) and section 96 were read without reference to paragraph 96(1)(d) is deemed to be proceeds of disposition that become receivable by the taxpayer at the end of the fiscal period in respect of a disposition of the property by the taxpayer.
Subsection 59(3.2) of the Act is amended by adding the following after paragraph (c):

Paragraph.Label: (c.1)
Paragraph.Text: any amount referred to in subsection 66.21(3);

Subsection.Text: Subsection 59(5) of the Act is replaced by the following:
In this section, proceeds of disposition has the meaning assigned by section 54.
Subsection 59(1) of the Act, as enacted by subsection (1), and subsection (2) apply to taxation years that begin after 2000.
Subsection 59(1.1) of the Act, as enacted by subsection (1), applies to fiscal periods that begin after 2000.
Subsection (3) applies to transactions and events that occur after December 23, 1998.
Section 60 of the Act is amended by adding the following after paragraph (d):

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: 1/2 of the lesser of

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the total of all amounts each of which is an amount payable by the taxpayer in respect of self-employed earnings for the year as a contribution under the Canada Pension Plan or under a provincial pension plan within the meaning assigned by section 3 of that Act, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the maximum amount of such contributions payable by the taxpayer for the year under the plan;

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) applies to the 2001 and subsequent taxation years.
Paragraph 63(1)(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: by the taxpayer, where the taxpayer is described in subsection (2) and the supporting person of the child for the year is a person described in clause (i)(D) of the description of C in the formula in that subsection, or

Subsection.Text: Subparagraph 63(1)(e)(ii) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the total of all amounts each of which is the annual child care expense amount in respect of an eligible child of the taxpayer for the year

Subsection.Text: The formula in paragraph 63(2)(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:
The descriptions of A and B in paragraph 63(2)(b) of the Act are replaced by the following:

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the total of all amounts each of which is the periodic child care expense amount in respect of an eligible child of the taxpayer for the year, and

Subsection.Text: The formula in paragraph 63(2.3)(c) of the Act is replaced by the following:
The descriptions of A and B in paragraph 63(2.3)(c) of the Act are replaced by the following:

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the total of all amounts each of which is the periodic child care expense amount in respect of an eligible child of the taxpayer for the year, and

Subsection.Text: Paragraph (c) of the definition child care expense in subsection 63(3) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: any such expenses paid in the year for a child’s attendance at a boarding school or camp to the extent that the total of those expenses exceeds the product obtained when the periodic child care expense amount in respect of the child for the year is multiplied by the number of weeks in the year during which the child attended the school or camp, and

Subsection.Text: Paragraph (b) of the definition earned income in subsection 63(3) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: all amounts that are included, or that would, but for paragraph 81(1)(a) or subsection 81(4), be included, because of section 6 or 7 or paragraph 56(1)(n), (o) or (r), in computing the taxpayer’s income,

Subsection.Text: Subsection 63(3) of the Act is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:

MarginalNote.DefinedTermFr: montant annuel de frais de garde d’enfants

Definition.Text: annual child care expense amount, in respect of an eligible child of a taxpayer for a taxation year, means

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: $10,000, where the child is a person in respect of whom an amount may be deducted under section 118.3 in computing a taxpayer’s tax payable under this Part for the year, and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: where the child is not a person referred to in paragraph (a),

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: $7,000, where the child is under 7 years of age at the end of the year, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: $4,000, in any other case;

MarginalNote.DefinedTermFr: montant périodique de frais de garde d’enfants

Definition.Text: periodic child care expense amount, in respect of an eligible child of a taxpayer for a taxation year, means 1/40 of the annual child care expense amount in respect of the child for the year;

Subsection.Text: Subsections (1) and (8) apply to the 1998 and subsequent taxation years.
Subsections (2) to (7) and (9) apply to the 2000 and subsequent taxation years.
Subparagraph (i) of the description of A in paragraph 64(a) of the Act is amended by striking out the word “or” at the end of clause (B) and by adding the following after clause (B):

FormulaParagraph.Label: (C)
FormulaParagraph.Text: attend a designated educational institution or a secondary school at which the taxpayer is enrolled in an educational program, or

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 64(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: 2/3 of the total of

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the total of all amounts each of which is

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: an amount included under section 5, 6 or 7 or paragraph 56(1)(n), (o) or (r) in computing the taxpayer’s income for the year, or

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: the taxpayer’s income for the year from a business carried on either alone or as a partner actively engaged in the business, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: where the taxpayer is in attendance at a designated educational institution or a secondary school at which the taxpayer is enrolled in an educational program, the least of

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: $375 times the number of weeks in the year during which the taxpayer is in attendance at the institution or school, and

Clause.Label: (C)
Clause.Text: the amount, if any, by which the amount that would, if this Act were read without reference to this section, be the taxpayer’s income for the year exceeds the total determined under subparagraph (i) in respect of the taxpayer for the year.

Subsection.Text: Subsections (1) and (2) apply to the 2000 and subsequent taxation years.
Subparagraph 66(4)(a)(i) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the total of the foreign exploration and development expenses incurred by the taxpayer

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: before the end of the year,

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: at a time at which the taxpayer was resident in Canada, and

Clause.Label: (C)
Clause.Text: where the taxpayer became resident in Canada before the end of the year, after the last time (before the end of the year) that the taxpayer became resident in Canada,

Subsection.Text: The portion of paragraph 66(4)(b) of the Act before subparagraph (ii) is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: of that total, the greater of

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the amount, if any, claimed by the taxpayer not exceeding 10% of the amount determined under paragraph (a) in respect of the taxpayer for the year, and

Subsection.Text: Subparagraph 66(4)(b)(ii) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the total of

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: the part of the taxpayer’s income for the year, determined without reference to this subsection and subsection 66.21(4), that can reasonably be regarded as attributable to

Subclause.Label: (I)
Subclause.Text: the production of petroleum or natural gas from natural accumulations outside Canada or from oil or gas wells outside Canada, or

Subclause.Label: (II)
Subclause.Text: the production of minerals from mines outside Canada,

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: the taxpayer’s income for the year from royalties in respect of a natural accumulation of petroleum or natural gas outside Canada, an oil or gas well outside Canada or a mine outside Canada, determined without reference to this subsection and subsection 66.21(4), and

Clause.Label: (C)
Clause.Text: all amounts each of which is an amount, in respect of a foreign resource property that has been disposed of by the taxpayer, equal to the amount, if any, by which

Subclause.Label: (I)
Subclause.Text: the amount included in computing the taxpayer’s income for the year by reason of subsection 59(1) in respect of the disposition

Subclause.Label: (II)
Subclause.Text: the total of all amounts each of which is that portion of an amount deducted under subsection 66.7(2) in computing the taxpayer’s income for the year that

Subsubclause.Text: can reasonably be considered to be in respect of the foreign resource property, and
cannot reasonably be considered to have reduced the amount otherwise determined under clause (A) or (B) in respect of the taxpayer for the year.

Subsection.Text: Section 66 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (4):

For greater certainty, the portion of an amount deducted under subsection (4) in computing a taxpayer’s income for a taxation year that can reasonably be considered to be in respect of specified foreign exploration and development expenses of the taxpayer in respect of a country is considered to apply to a source in that country.

For the purpose of subsection (4.1), where a taxpayer has incurred specified foreign exploration and development expenses in respect of two or more countries, an allocation to each of those countries for a taxation year shall be determined in a manner that is

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: reasonable having regard to all the circumstances, including the level and timing of

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the taxpayer’s specified foreign exploration and development expenses in respect of the country, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the profits or gains to which those expenses relate; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: not inconsistent with the allocation made under subsection (4.1) for the preceding taxation year.

Subsection.MarginalNote: FEDE deductions where change of individual’s residence
Subsection.Text: Where at any time in a taxation year an individual becomes or ceases to be resident in Canada,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: subsection (4) applies to the individual as if the year were the period or periods in the year throughout which the individual was resident in Canada; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: for the purpose of applying subsection (4), subsection (13.1) does not apply to the individual for the year.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 66(5) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsections (3) and (4) and sections 59, 64, 66.1, 66.2, 66.21, 66.4 and 66.7 do not apply in computing the income for a taxation year of a taxpayer (other than a principal-business corporation) whose business includes trading or dealing in rights, licences or privileges to explore for, drill for or take minerals, petroleum, natural gas or other related hydrocarbons.
The portion of subsection 66(11.4) of the Act after paragraph (c) is replaced by the following:

ContinuedSectionSubsection.Text: for the purposes of subsection (4) and sections 66.2, 66.21 and 66.4, except as those provisions apply for the purposes of section 66.7, the property is deemed not to have been acquired by the corporation or partnership before that time and is deemed to have been acquired by it at that time, except that, where the property has been disposed of by it before that time and not reacquired by it before that time, the property is deemed to have been acquired by the corporation or partnership immediately before it disposed of the property.

Subsection.Text: The portion of subsection 66(12.4) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Where, as a result of a transaction that occurs after May 6, 1974, an amount becomes receivable by a taxpayer at a particular time in a taxation year and the consideration given by the taxpayer for the amount receivable is property (other than a foreign resource property) or services, the original cost of which to the taxpayer can reasonably be regarded as having been primarily foreign exploration and development expenses of the taxpayer (or would have been so regarded if they had been incurred by the taxpayer after 1971 and the definition foreign exploration and development expenses in subsection (15) were read without reference to paragraph (k) of that definition), the following rules apply:
Paragraph 66(12.4)(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: where the amount receivable exceeds the total of the taxpayer’s foreign exploration and development expenses incurred before that time to the extent that those expenses were not deducted or deductible, as the case may be, in computing the taxpayer’s income for a preceding taxation year, there shall be included in the amount referred to in paragraph 59(3.2)(a) the amount, if any, by which the amount receivable exceeds the total of

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the taxpayer’s foreign exploration and development expenses incurred before that time to the extent that those expenses were not deducted or deductible, as the case may be, in computing the taxpayer’s income for a preceding taxation year, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the amount, designated by the taxpayer in prescribed form filed with the taxpayer’s return of income for the year, not exceeding the portion of the amount receivable for which the consideration given by the taxpayer was property (other than a foreign resource property) or services, the original cost of which to the taxpayer can reasonably be regarded as having been primarily

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: specified foreign exploration and development expenses in respect of a country, or

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: foreign resource expenses in respect of a country; and

Subsection.Text: Section 66 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (12.4):

Where a particular amount described in subsection (12.4) becomes receivable by a taxpayer at a particular time, there shall at that time be included in the value determined for G in the definition cumulative foreign resource expense in subsection 66.21(1) in respect of the taxpayer and a country the amount designated under subparagraph (12.4)(b)(ii) by the taxpayer in respect of the particular amount and the country.

For the purposes of subsections (12.4) and (12.41), where a person or partnership is a member of a particular partnership and a particular amount described in subsection (12.4) becomes receivable by the particular partnership in a fiscal period of the particular partnership,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the member’s share of the particular amount is deemed to be an amount that became receivable by the member at the end of the fiscal period; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the amount deemed by paragraph (a) to be an amount receivable by the member is deemed to be an amount

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: that is described in subsection (12.4) in respect of the member, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: that has the same attributes for the member as it did for the particular partnership.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 66(13.1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Where a taxpayer has a taxation year that is less than 51 weeks, the amount determined in respect of the year under each of subparagraph (4)(b)(i), paragraph 66.2(2)(c), subparagraph (b)(i) of the definition global foreign resource limit in subsection 66.21(1), subparagraph 66.21(4)(a)(i), clause 66.21(4)(a)(ii)(B) and paragraphs 66.4(2)(b) and 66.7(2.3)(a), (4)(a) and (5)(a) shall not exceed that proportion of the amount otherwise determined that the number of days in the year is of 365.
The definitions original owner and predecessor owner in subsection 66(15) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Definition.Text: original owner of a Canadian resource property or a foreign resource property means a person

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: who owned the property and disposed of it to a corporation that acquired it in circumstances in which subsection 29(25) of the Income Tax Application Rules or subsection 66.7(1), (2), (2.3), (3), (4) or (5) applies, or would apply if the corporation had continued to own the property, to the corporation in respect of the property, and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: who would, but for subsection 66.7(12), (13), (13.1) or (17), as the case may be, be entitled in computing that person’s income for a taxation year that ends after that person disposed of the property to a deduction under section 29 of the Income Tax Application Rules or subsection (2), (3) or (4), 66.1(2) or (3), 66.2(2), 66.21(4) or 66.4(2) of this Act in respect of expenses described in subparagraph 29(25)(c)(i) or (ii) of that Act, Canadian exploration and development expenses, foreign resource pool expenses, Canadian exploration expenses, Canadian development expenses or Canadian oil and gas property expenses incurred by the person before the person disposed of the property;

Definition.Text: predecessor owner of a Canadian resource property or a foreign resource property means a corporation

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: that acquired the property in circumstances in which subsection 29(25) of the Income Tax Application Rules or subsection 66.7(1), (2), (2.3), (3), (4) or (5) applies, or would apply if the corporation had continued to own the property, to the corporation in respect of the property,

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: that disposed of the property to another corporation that acquired it in circumstances in which subsection 29(25) of the Income Tax Application Rules or subsection 66.7(1), (2), (2.3), (3), (4) or (5) applies, or would apply if the other corporation had continued to own the property, to the other corporation in respect of the property, and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: that would, but for subsection 66.7(14), (15), (15.1) or (17), as the case may be, be entitled in computing its income for a taxation year ending after it disposed of the property to a deduction under subsection 29(25) of the Income Tax Application Rules or subsection 66.7(1), (2), (2.3), (3), (4) or (5) in respect of expenses incurred by an original owner of the property;

Subsection.Text: Paragraph (c) of the definition Canadian resource property in subsection 66(15) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: any oil or gas well in Canada or any real property in Canada the principal value of which depends on its petroleum or natural gas content (but not including any depreciable property),

Subsection.Text: Paragraph (f) of the definition Canadian resource property in subsection 66(15) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: any real property in Canada the principal value of which depends on its mineral resource content (but not including any depreciable property), or

Subsection.Text: Paragraph (b) of the definition foreign exploration and development expenses in subsection 66(15) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: any expense incurred by the taxpayer for the purpose of determining the existence, location, extent or quality of a mineral resource outside Canada, including any expense incurred in the course of

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: prospecting,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: carrying out geological, geophysical or geochemical surveys,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: drilling by rotary, diamond, percussion or other method, or

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: trenching, digging test pits and preliminary sampling,

Subsection.Text: The definition foreign exploration and development expenses in subsection 66(15) of the Act is amended by striking out the word “or” at the end of paragraph (h) and by adding the following after paragraph (i):

Paragraph.Label: (j)
Paragraph.Text: an expenditure that is the cost, or any part of the cost, to the taxpayer of any depreciable property of a prescribed class that was acquired after December 21, 2000,

Paragraph.Label: (k)
Paragraph.Text: foreign resource expenses in respect of a country, or

Paragraph.Label: (l)
Paragraph.Text: an expenditure made after February 27, 2000 by the taxpayer unless the expenditure was made

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: pursuant to an agreement in writing made by the taxpayer before February 28, 2000,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: for the acquisition of foreign resource property by the taxpayer, or

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: for the purpose of

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: enhancing the value of foreign resource property that the taxpayer owned at the time the expenditure was incurred or that the taxpayer had a reasonable expectation of owning after that time, or

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: assisting in evaluating whether a foreign resource property is to be acquired by the taxpayer;

Subsection.Text: Subsection 66(15) of the Act is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:

MarginalNote.DefinedTermEn: specified foreign exploration and development expense

MarginalNote.DefinedTermFr: frais d’exploration et d’aménagement à l’étranger déterminés

Definition.Text: specified foreign exploration and development expense of a taxpayer in respect of a country (other than Canada) means an amount that is included in the taxpayer’s foreign exploration and development expenses and that is

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a drilling or exploration expense, including any general geological or geophysical expense, incurred by the taxpayer on or in respect of exploring or drilling for petroleum or natural gas in that country,

Paragraph.Label: (a.1)
Paragraph.Text: an expense incurred by the taxpayer after December 21, 2000 (otherwise than pursuant to an agreement in writing made before December 22, 2000) for the purpose of determining the existence, location, extent or quality of a mineral resource in that country, including any expense incurred in the course of

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: prospecting,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: carrying out geological, geophysical or geochemical surveys,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: drilling by rotary, diamond, percussion or other methods, or

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: trenching, digging test pits and preliminary sampling,

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a prospecting, exploration or development expense incurred by the taxpayer before December 22, 2000 (or after December 21, 2000 pursuant to an agreement in writing made before December 22, 2000) in searching for minerals in that country,

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the cost to the taxpayer of the taxpayer’s foreign resource property in respect of that country,

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: an annual payment made by the taxpayer in a taxation year of the taxpayer for the preservation of a foreign resource property in respect of that country,

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: an amount deemed by subsection 21(2) or (4) to be a foreign exploration and development expense incurred by the taxpayer, to the extent that it can reasonably be considered to relate to an amount that, without reference to this paragraph and paragraph (f), would be a specified foreign exploration and development expense in respect of that country, or

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: subject to section 66.8, the taxpayer’s share of the specified foreign exploration and development expenses of a partnership incurred in respect of that country in a fiscal period of the partnership if, at the end of that period, the taxpayer was a member of the partnership.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 66(15.1) of the Act is replaced by the following:
The definitions in subsections 66.1(6), 66.2(5), 66.21(1), 66.4(5) and 66.5(2) apply in this section.
Subsection 66(18) of the Act is replaced by the following:

For the purposes of this section, subsection 21(2), sections 59.1 and 66.1 to 66.7, paragraph (d) of the definition investment expense in subsection 110.6(1) and the descriptions of C and D in subsection 211.91(1), where a person’s share of an outlay or expense made or incurred by a partnership in a fiscal period of the partnership is included in respect of the person under paragraph (d) of the definition foreign exploration and development expenses in subsection (15), paragraph (h) of the definition Canadian exploration expense in subsection 66.1(6), paragraph (f) of the definition Canadian development expense in subsection 66.2(5), paragraph (e) of the definition foreign resource expense in subsection 66.21(1) or paragraph (b) of the definition Canadian oil and gas property expense in subsection 66.4(5), the portion of the outlay or expense so included is deemed, except for the purposes of applying the definitions foreign exploration and development expenses, Canadian exploration expense, Canadian development expense, foreign resource expense and Canadian oil and gas property expense in respect of the person, to be made or incurred by the person at the end of that fiscal period.

Subsection (1) applies to the 1999 and subsequent taxation years except that in its application to the 1999 taxation year, subparagraph 66(4)(a)(i) of the Act, as enacted by subsection (1), shall be read as follows:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the total of the foreign exploration and development expenses incurred by the taxpayer before the end of the year and at a time which the taxpayer was resident in Canada

Subsection.Text: Subsection (2) applies to the 1995 and subsequent taxation years, except that the portion of paragraph 66(4)(b) of the Act before subparagraph (ii), as enacted by subsection (2), shall be read as follows in respect of cessations of residence that occurred before February 28, 2000:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: of that total, the greatest of

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the amount, if any, claimed by the taxpayer not exceeding 10% of the amount determined under paragraph (a) in respect of the taxpayer for the year,

Subparagraph.Label: (i.1)
Subparagraph.Text: if the taxpayer ceased to be resident in Canada immediately after the end of the year, the amount, if any, claimed by the taxpayer not exceeding the amount determined under paragraph (a) in respect of the taxpayer for the year, and

Subsection.Text: Subsections (3) and (5) to (8), subsection 66(12.41) of the Act, as enacted by subsection (9), subsections (10) to (13) and paragraph (k) of the definition foreign exploration and development expenses in subsection 66(15) of the Act, as enacted by subsection (15), apply to taxation years that begin after 2000.
Subsections 66(4.1) and (4.2) of the Act, as enacted by subsection (4), apply to taxation years of a taxpayer that begin after the earlier of

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: December 31, 1999; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: where, for the purposes of subsection 117(26), a date is designated in writing by the taxpayer and the designation is filed with the Minister of National Revenue on or before the taxpayer’s filing-due date for the taxpayer’s taxation year that includes the day on which this Act receives royal assent, the later of

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the date so designated, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: December 31, 1994.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 66(4.3) of the Act, as enacted by subsection (4), applies to the 1998 and subsequent taxation years.
Subsection 66(12.42) of the Act, as enacted by subsection (9), and subsection (18) apply to fiscal periods that begin after 2000.
Subsection (14) applies to expenses incurred after December 21, 2000, other than expenses incurred pursuant to an agreement in writing made before December 22, 2000.
Paragraph (j) of the definition foreign exploration and development expenses in subsection 66(15) of the Act, as enacted by subsection (15), and subsection (17) apply after 2000.
Paragraph (l) of the definition foreign exploration and development expenses in subsection 66(15) of the Act, as enacted by subsection (15), applies after February 27, 2000.
Subsection (16) applies after 1994.
Subparagraph (d)(i) of the definition Canadian exploration expense in subsection 66.1(6) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the drilling or completing of the well resulted in the discovery that a natural underground reservoir contains petroleum or natural gas, where

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: before the time of the discovery, no person or partnership had discovered that the reservoir contained either petroleum or natural gas, and

Clause.Label: (B)
